title: physostigma laxius
intro: Physostigma laxius, also known as the Calabar bean, is a plant species native to West Africa and parts of tropical Africa. The plant contains a compound called physostigmine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to dilate blood vessels and stimulate the nervous system. The health benefits of Physostigma laxius include its potential use in treating conditions such as glaucoma, myasthenia gravis, and Parkinson's disease, as well as its ability to improve cognitive function and memory. The herbal preparation of Physostigma laxius is typically made by crushing the seeds and mixing them with water or other solvents to extract the physostigmine, which can then be used to create various medicinal preparations.
